---
title: "Mesfin Genie"
page-layout: full
description: "Mesfin Genie is a Senior Lecturer in Health Economics at the University of Newcastle."
image: assets/mesfin-genie-profile.webp
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.page-shell .hero-grid}
::: {.portrait-shell}
![](assets/mesfin-genie-profile.webp){.profile-photo alt="Mesfin Genie"}
:::

::: {.hero-content}
[Open to collaboration]{.availability-pill}

# Mesfin Genie

<div class="hero-role">Senior Lecturer in Health Economics</div>
<div class="hero-affiliation">Newcastle Business School · The University of Newcastle</div>

I study how patients and the public make trade-offs in healthcare and public policy. My work combines health economics, choice modelling, behavioural decision science, eye tracking and applied econometrics.

::: {.hero-actions}
[Publications](/publications/){.button-primary}
[Curriculum vitae](/cv/){.button-secondary}
:::

::: {.social-row aria-label="Academic and professional profiles"}
<a class="social-link" href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=v2SXM_kAAAAJ&hl=en" title="Google Scholar" aria-label="Google Scholar"><i class="ai ai-google-scholar"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1744-4666" title="ORCID" aria-label="ORCID"><i class="ai ai-orcid"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Mesfin-Genie" title="ResearchGate" aria-label="ResearchGate"><i class="ai ai-researchgate"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://au.linkedin.com/in/mesfin-genie-ph-d-65634216" title="LinkedIn" aria-label="LinkedIn"><i class="bi bi-linkedin"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://github.com/DrGenie" title="GitHub" aria-label="GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://www.newcastle.edu.au/profile/mesfin-genie" title="University profile" aria-label="University profile"><i class="bi bi-building"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="mailto:mesfin.genie@newcastle.edu.au" title="Email" aria-label="Email"><i class="bi bi-envelope"></i></a>
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section}
::: {.page-shell}
## Research

::: {.research-strip}
::: {.research-card}
### Health preferences and choice modelling

Patient and public preferences, discrete choice experiments, preference heterogeneity, equity and value assessment.
:::

::: {.research-card}
### Decision processes

Attention, information processing, numeracy, feedback, attribute ordering and eye-tracking evidence.
:::

::: {.research-card}
### Public health policy

Vaccination, mandates, telehealth, preventive care and economic evaluation of health programmes.
:::
:::

[Research programme →](/research/){.text-link}
:::
:::

::: {.section .section-tint}
::: {.page-shell}
::: {.section-heading-row}
## Recent publications
[All publications →](/publications/){.text-link}
:::

::: {.featured-publications}
::: {.featured-publication}
<div class="publication-eyebrow">Social Science &amp; Medicine · 2026</div>

### [Fit for purpose? Assessing the robustness of discrete choice experiment designs in the context of evolving health technologies](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.socscimed.2026.119463){target="_blank"}

**Mesfin G. Genie**, Verity Watson, Michaël Schwarzinger and Stéphane Luchini

[Published]{.status}
:::

::: {.featured-publication}
<div class="publication-eyebrow">Health Economics · 2026</div>

### [Guidance or misdirection? Unpacking the role of feedback in health preference assessments](https://doi.org/10.1002/hec.70093){target="_blank"}

**Mesfin G. Genie**, Shelby D. Reed and Semra Ozdemir

[Published]{.status}
:::

::: {.featured-publication}
<div class="publication-eyebrow">Value in Health · 2026</div>

### [Priority for self or others? Incorporating equity considerations in a preference-based health value assessment](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jval.2025.10.011){target="_blank"}

**Mesfin G. Genie** and Surachat Ngorsuraches

[Published]{.status}
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section}
::: {.page-shell}
::: {.section-heading-row}
## Tools
[All tools →](/tools/){.text-link}
:::

::: {.tool-grid}
::: {.tool-card}
<div class="tool-topline"><span>Vaccine policy</span><span class="live-pill">Live</span></div>

### eMANDEVA Decision Aid

Public support, lives saved, benefit-cost outcomes and policy-design trade-offs for prospective vaccine mandates.

[Launch eMANDEVA ↗](https://mesfingenie.com/eMANDEVA-DecisionAid-V18/){.tool-link target="_blank"}
:::

::: {.tool-card}
<div class="tool-topline"><span>Economic evaluation</span><span class="live-pill">Live</span></div>

### Farming BCA Tool

Net present value, benefit-cost ratio, return on investment and sensitivity analysis for farm practices and soil-management interventions.

[Launch Farming BCA Tool ↗](https://mesfingenie.com/farming-bca-tool-v18/){.tool-link target="_blank"}
:::
:::
:::
:::
